text.title: PLG Basics Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every application of `PLG Basics` must enforce. Each rule pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale.

rule.statement: The spec MUST pick one of: freemium / free-trial / reverse-trial / open-source with explicit rationale referencing product shape + ICP + competitive context. 'We'll do freemium + trial' (both at once) is forbidden until 12+ months post-launch data exists.
rule.rationale: Mixed models confuse intent + dilute focus; pick one and iterate before adding a second.

rule.statement: Free-tier limits MUST be explicit (seats / projects / API calls / storage) with upgrade triggers defined. 'Generous free tier' is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Vague limits cause power-users to never upgrade; explicit limits + upgrade triggers create upgrade pressure.

rule.statement: Checkout MUST work end-to-end self-serve (signup → trial → paid → expansion) without sales touch up to a defined ARR tier (typically $5K-$10K/year). Sales touch below the tier is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Sales touch on small accounts drains rep capacity; pinned threshold enforces self-serve discipline.

rule.statement: Activation TTV (time signup → first value) MUST be measured + pinned (e.g. < 5 min for prosumer SaaS). Unmeasured TTV is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Without TTV measurement, the team optimises in the dark; the named floor anchors the activation work.

rule.statement: Product-Qualified Lead criteria MUST be defined before PLG launch (e.g. user invited 3+ teammates AND used X feature 5+ times). Post-hoc PQL scoring is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Post-hoc PQL definitions overfit; pre-launch criteria force the team to think through the conversion logic.

rule.statement: When preconditions in `Applies If` fail OR any item in `Skip If` triggers, do not run this methodology. Document the skip rationale in the artefact's parent log.
rule.rationale: Misapplied methodologies are worse than no methodology — they consume review cycles and produce false confidence.
